findings.title: serverStatuses stored only in reactive ref, not persisted
findings.description: The serverStatuses map is a Vue reactive ref that resets to empty on page reload. While server configs are persisted via useLocalStorageManualReset, the connection statuses are ephemeral. This is acceptable for transient connection state IF there is a mechanism to re-derive statuses on startup by probing each server. However, no such reconnection/probe logic exists in this store -- statuses will always show 'disconnected' after reload, even if servers are actually running. The updateStatus function is exposed but never called from any wiring code in this diff.
findings.suggestion: Add an init/reconnect action to the store that probes each enabled server's actual status on mount (e.g., via McpHub.connectEnabled or a health ping). Wire this from the settings page or app bootstrap. Alternatively, accept that statuses are always 'disconnected' on load and document that the backend owns the real connection state.

findings.title: createAnimaMcpServer exported but not consumed by any entry point
findings.description: createAnimaMcpServer is exported from packages/mcp-hub/src/index.ts and tested, but there is no consumer anywhere in the codebase that imports and calls it. The AnimaMemoryAccess and AnimaContextAccess interfaces define contracts against context-engine, but no wiring in apps/stage-tamagotchi or any other entry point instantiates this server with real deps. This is orphan code -- it cannot be reached from any live entry point (HTTP handler, Electron main, CLI, etc.).
findings.suggestion: Wire createAnimaMcpServer in the Electron main process service layer (e.g., apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/main/services/anima/) with real AnimaMemoryAccess/AnimaContextAccess implementations from context-engine. If context-engine is not ready, define a stub with a TODO-free rationale comment and create a follow-up task.

findings.title: UI store broadcasts MCP config but no integration test validates the contract
findings.description: The MCP module store broadcasts configuration via configurator.updateFor('mcp', ...) which sends a WebSocket message with shape { moduleName: 'mcp', config: { servers: McpServerConfigInput[] } }. There is no integration test or contract test proving that the backend (server-runtime or mcp-hub) understands and correctly processes this message shape. The toBackendConfig function maps McpServerUiConfig to McpServerConfigInput, but the contract between the UI and backend is unverified.
findings.suggestion: Add a contract test (even a simple shape validation test) that validates the output of toBackendConfig matches the expected McpServerConfigInput discriminated union from @proj-airi/mcp-hub. Alternatively, add an E2E test with a mock WebSocket server verifying the message format.

findings.title: addedRecommendedIds matches by name instead of id, causing false positives
findings.description: The computed set addedRecommendedIds collects server names but is checked against rec.name (the recommended server's display name). If a user manually adds a server named 'Filesystem', it would incorrectly disable the recommended 'Filesystem' server button even though the manual server is unrelated. Additionally, if a user renames an added recommended server, the recommended button would become re-enabled allowing a duplicate. The correct approach is to track by a stable identifier.
findings.suggestion: Track which recommended server IDs have been added. Either: (1) store the original recommendedId on McpServerUiConfig when added via addFromRecommended, or (2) match by both transport + command/url signature rather than display name. For example: `new Set(servers.value.filter(s => s.recommendedSourceId).map(s => s.recommendedSourceId))`.

findings.title: No validation on URL/command inputs before saving MCP server config
findings.description: The handleAdd function only validates that the server name is non-empty. For stdio transport, it does not check that the command field is non-empty. For http/sse transport, it does not validate that the URL field is non-empty or has a valid URL format. A user could save a server with transport='stdio' and an empty command, or transport='http' with an empty/malformed URL. The toBackendConfig function will produce { command: '' } or { url: '' } which may cause silent failures downstream.
findings.suggestion: Add validation before saving: for stdio servers require a non-empty command, for http/sse servers require a non-empty URL matching a URL pattern (e.g., starts with http:// or https://). Show validation errors in the form UI.

findings.title: No logging in anima-mcp-server tool/resource handlers
findings.code_snippet:
export function createAnimaMcpServer(deps: AnimaMcpServerDeps): McpServer {
  // ... no logging in any handler
findings.description: The createAnimaMcpServer function registers two tools and two resources, but none of the handlers include any logging. When an external client calls search_memories or get_daily_summary, or reads the user-context/memories resources, there is no observability into what was requested, how long it took, or whether it succeeded. This is an MCP server that external tools (Claude Desktop, etc.) connect to -- failures will be invisible.
findings.suggestion: Add @guiiai/logg useLogg calls at the start and completion of each tool/resource handler, logging the query parameters and result count (not full content). Example: logger.info('search_memories called', { query, limit, resultCount: results.length }).

findings.title: MCP settings page route exists but no backend handler processes 'mcp' config
findings.description: The MCP settings page, store, and Vue component form a horizontal UI-only slice. The broadcastConfig() call sends a WebSocket message with moduleName='mcp', but there is no corresponding handler in server-runtime or any backend service that processes this message. The UI allows adding/editing/removing servers, but the configuration never reaches the McpHub class that actually manages connections. This change delivers a UI panel without a working end-to-end data path.
findings.suggestion: Add a handler in the server-runtime (or Electron main process IPC layer) that receives 'ui:configure' messages with moduleName='mcp' and routes them to McpHub.addServer/updateServer/removeServer. This closes the vertical slice from UI to backend.

findings.title: Export name McpServer for settings component is misleading
findings.description: The component is exported as 'McpServer' but the file is named 'McpManager.vue' and it renders a settings management panel, not an MCP server. The name 'McpServer' could be confused with the actual McpServer class from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. Other settings components in the same barrel export use descriptive names (Bar, ButtonBar, CheckBar).
findings.suggestion: Rename the export to McpManager or McpSettings for clarity: `export { default as McpManager } from './McpManager.vue'`. Update the import in mcp.vue page accordingly.

positive_observations: Clean contract-first design: AnimaMemoryAccess and AnimaContextAccess interfaces define clear boundaries between the MCP protocol adapter and the storage layer, enabling proper dependency injection.

Test quality for anima-mcp-server is excellent: uses real InMemoryTransport from the MCP SDK to test the full protocol round-trip (register tool -> connect -> call tool -> parse response), with proper Test Double rationale comment.
Server config persistence uses useLocalStorageManualReset, correctly persisting user-configured servers across page reloads.
The recommended-servers module is pure data with a clean getter function, easy to test and extend.
The McpManager.vue component follows project conventions: UnoCSS v-bind class arrays, Iconify icons, proper i18n with vue-i18n, and idiomatic Vue 3 Composition API.
